EvaluateMLModelPiece.name: EvaluateMLModelPiece
EvaluateMLModelPiece.description: Template piece for evaluating a trained ML model and producing metrics/artifacts.

payload.description:
Arbitrary inputs as JSON object.
Supported evaluation keys (optional):
- `evaluation_option`: 'normal' or 'errorcorrection' (default: 'normal').
- `pred_df`: predictions DataFrame (required for evaluation).
- `true_baseline_df`: baseline DataFrame (used for errorcorrection when `y_true` isn't provided).
- `y_true`: array/series of true values (used for errorcorrection test split).
- `baseline_id`: baseline pred_sequence_id (default: 1).
- `plot`: bool (default: false; heatmaps only generated when true).
payload.title: Payload

artifacts.description: Optional outputs (e.g., metrics JSON, plots, report URI).
artifacts.title: Artifacts

DataNormalizationPiece.name: DataNormalizationPiece
DataNormalizationPiece.description: Template piece for normalizing raw smart-grid data into a consistent scale and schema.

payload.description: JSON object with arbitrary inputs. For DataNormalizationPiece, expected keys include: `dataframe` (dataframe-like object), `type` (normalization type), and optional `features` (list of column names to normalize).
payload.title: Payload

artifacts.description: Optional outputs (e.g., normalized dataset URI, fitted scaler params).
artifacts.title: Artifacts

SyntheticDataGeneratorPiece.name: SyntheticDataGeneratorPiece
SyntheticDataGeneratorPiece.description: Generate synthetic tabular datasets for multiple smart-grid data domains in batch or realtime-like mode.

payload.description:
Synthetic data generation config.
Expected keys:
- `dataset_type`: one of `solargis`, `microstep`, `shmu`, `okte`, `battery`, `machine`.
- `output_mode`: `batch_sample` or `realtime_stream`.
- `records_count`: number of generated rows (default 20).
- `time_step_minutes`: step between timestamps (default 15).
- `interval_ms`: realtime interval hint in milliseconds (default 1000).
- Optional: `start_at` (ISO datetime), `seed` (int), `timezone_offset_hours` (number).
payload.title: Payload

InferencePiece.name: InferencePiece
InferencePiece.description: Runs inference on prepared features: pvout_correction or price_ahead (base + model correction).

payload.description:
Inference inputs as JSON object. For a no-op run, pass an empty payload, or omit both `mode` and `stages`.
MVP keys when running inference:
- `mode`: `pvout_correction`, `price_ahead` (baseline + correction), or `price_level` (direct regression output, e.g. EUR/MWh from electricity price XGB model).
- `model_path`: path to `.pkl` (joblib) or xgboost booster file.
- `input`: `{ "tabular_data": [...] }` or `{ "data_path": "..." }` (CSV/parquet).
- `datetime_column` (default `datetime`), `feature_columns` (or from preprocessing metadata).
- `base_forecast_column`: e.g. `PVOUT` or `price_baseline`.
- `horizon_column` (default `pred_sequence_id`), optional `max_horizon`.
- Optional: `preprocessing_metadata_path`, `strict_schema`, `missing_fill_value`, `per_horizon_outputs`, `forecast_output_csv_path`, `return_debug`.
- Price mode: `build_baseline_if_missing` + `price_profile_path` (CSV with `dow`, `slot_15m`, `avg_price_eur_mwh`) when baseline column is missing.
Upstream data fetching (SolarGis, weather, OKTE) is expected in separate pipeline steps; this piece consumes prepared tabular features.
payload.title: Payload

artifacts.description: No-op: `input_payload`. Run: `forecast` (inline_records, csv_path, columns), `per_horizon`, `metadata`, optional `debug`. Pipeline: additionally `stage_summaries`.
artifacts.title: Artifacts

DataPreprocessingPiece.name: DataPreprocessingPiece
DataPreprocessingPiece.description: Template piece for cleaning, validating, and preprocessing input data prior to feature engineering or training.

payload.description:
Preprocessing inputs as JSON object. Expected keys:
- `preprocessing_option`: one of `none`, `prediction`, `correction` (default: `none`).
- For `prediction`: provide either `dataframe` (preferred) or `data_path`, plus `save_data_path` (optional), `flag_each_day`, `preprocessor_features`, and `keep_datetime`.
- For `correction`: provide either `dataframe` (preferred) or `data_path`, plus `save_data_path` (optional), `flag_each_day`, `preprocessor_features` and optional `test_size` / `load_all_data`.
payload.title: Payload

artifacts.description: Optional outputs (e.g., cleaned dataset URI, dropped rows stats).
artifacts.title: Artifacts

PVOUTErrorCorrectionModelTrainPiece.name: PVOUTErrorCorrectionModelTrainPiece
PVOUTErrorCorrectionModelTrainPiece.description: Template piece for training a PVOUT error-correction model.

payload.description:
Training inputs for PVOUT error-correction model.
Expected keys:
- `model_type`: one of `error_correction_xgb_regressor_model`, `error_correction_residual_meta_xgb_regressor_model`, `error_correction_difficulty_weighted_xgb_regressor_model` (and lightweight built-ins `linear_regression` / `ridge_regression`).
- `model_params`: constructor params passed to selected model.
- `model_setup`: dict with `feature_columns`, optional `target_column`, and `pred_column` for error-correction models.
- Input data provided by `data_path` (CSV) or `tabular_data` (list[dict] / dict-of-lists).
- Optional `checkpoint_dir` for saved model checkpoint.
payload.title: Payload

artifacts.description: Optional outputs (e.g., trained corrector URI, evaluation metrics).
artifacts.title: Artifacts

ElectricityPricePredictionModelTrainPiec.name: ElectricityPricePredictionModelTrainPiece
ElectricityPricePredictionModelTrainPiec.description: Template piece for training an electricity price prediction model.

payload.description:
Train an XGBoost regressor for electricity price (EUR/MWh) from tabular energy data.

**Input data** (one row per delivery interval, e.g. 15 min):
- `tabular_data` / `data_path` / `csv_path`: rows with energy-side features you already have (load, RES forecast, temperature, lags, calendar, etc.).
- `model_setup.datetime_column` (default `datetime`): wall time of the interval; naive values are interpreted as Europe/Bratislava when using OKTE.
- `model_setup.feature_columns` (required): numeric columns used as X.
- `model_setup.target_column` (default `price_eur_mwh`): regression target y.
- `model_setup.target_source`:
  - `column` — target must already be present in each row (historical prices from any source).
  - `okte` — fetch DAM prices from OKTE for each row's slot (optional `okte.endpoint`); writes `price_eur_mwh` and `price_source` before training.

**Training**:
- `xgb_params`: optional dict passed to `xgboost.XGBRegressor` (defaults are sensible for tabular).
- `output_dir`, `model_filename` (default `electricity_price_xgb.joblib`).
- `save_enriched_csv`: if true and `target_source=okte`, also write merged CSV for debugging.

**Outputs**: `model_path` (joblib), `preprocessing_metadata.json` path, `train_metrics`, `train_rows`, `fallback_used` (OKTE 28d fallback when applicable).
payload.title: Payload

PVOUTPredictionModelTrainPiece.name: PVOUTPredictionModelTrainPiece
PVOUTPredictionModelTrainPiece.description: Template piece for training a PVOUT prediction model.

payload.description:
Training inputs for PVOUT prediction model.
Expected keys:
- `model_type`: one of `linear_regression_model`, `xgb_regressor_model`, `interval_xgb_regressor_model`, `eda_rule_baseline`, `tabpfn_regressor_model`.
- `model_params`: constructor params for selected model.
- `model_setup`: dict with required `feature_columns` and optional `target_column`.
- Input data via `data_path` (CSV) or `tabular_data` (list[dict] / dict-of-lists).
- Optional `checkpoint_dir` for model checkpoint.
payload.title: Payload

artifacts.description: Optional outputs (e.g., trained model URI, training metrics).
artifacts.title: Artifacts

ModelDeciderPiece.name: ModelDeciderPiece
ModelDeciderPiece.description: Template piece for selecting a model/version based on context, data availability, or policies.

payload.description: Arbitrary inputs as JSON object (e.g., problem type, horizon, available models, constraints).
payload.title: Payload

artifacts.description: Optional outputs (e.g., selected model id/version, decision rationale).
artifacts.title: Artifacts

ExplainablePredictionPiece.name: ExplainablePredictionPiece
ExplainablePredictionPiece.description: Template piece for generating prediction explanations (e.g., feature attributions) alongside inference results.

payload.description:
Inputs for generating explanations as JSON object.
Optional keys:
- `model`: trained model object.
- `data`: evaluation dataset as `pd.DataFrame`, `(X, y)` tuple, or dict `{X, y?, feature_names?}`.
- `explainability`: `{method: 'lime'|'shap', mode: 'regression'|'classification', ...}`.
- `explain_method`: shortcut for `explainability.method`.
- `use_diagnostic_loss`: if true, build diagnostic heatmaps from `payload['diagnostic']`.
- `diagnostic`: dict with precomputed diagnostic arrays used by diagnostic heatmaps.
- `x_train`: optional DataFrame used to derive `hour_of_day` for heatmaps.
payload.title: Payload

artifacts.description: Optional outputs (e.g., explanation report URI, attribution arrays).
artifacts.title: Artifacts
